title: Effect of mindfulness yoga on anxiety and depression in early breast cancer patients received adjuvant chemotherapy: a randomized clinical trial
journal: Journal of cancer research and clinical oncology
headline: Effect of mindfulness yoga on anxiety and depression in early breast cancer patients received adjuvant chemoth
summary: A randomized controlled trial of yoga examined clinical research. The objective of this study is to explore the effects of a mindfulness yoga treatment on emotional disorders, fatigue, pain, and health-related quality of life in early-stage breast cancer patients.
keyFindings: The objective of this study is to explore the effects of a mindfulness yoga treatment on emotional disorders, fatigue, pain, and health-related quality of life in early-stage breast cancer patients.
The eligible 136 participants were randomly assigned 1:1 to the experimental group (mindfulness yoga + conventional care) and the control group (conventional care).
limitations: Single-trial result; requires replication, and self-reported outcomes may inflate apparent effects.

The practice studied (yoga) is not the Miracle of Mind practice, so its findings don't transfer directly — though the shared directed-attention component may make some transfer plausible (see “Why this matters for the 7-minute practice” below).
simplifiedTitle: Effect of mindfulness yoga on anxiety and depression in early breast cancer patients received adjuvant chemoth
journalName: Journal of cancer research and clinical oncology
aiSnippet: RCT, Journal of cancer research and clinical oncology (2022). The objective of this study is to explore the effects of a mindfulness yoga treatment on emotional disorders, fatigue, pain, and health-related quality of life in early-stage breast cancer patients. The eligible 136 participants were randomly assigned 1:1 to the experimental group (mindfulness yoga + conventional care) and the control group (conventional care).
whyItMatters: MoM shares directed attention (the focused-attention component) with the practice studied here. Because the benefit is linked to that mechanism rather than to any one method, it's reasonable to expect it may extend to MoM as well — though MoM hasn't been studied directly for this outcome.
